Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search emails for invoice corrections, surcharge approvals, and billing adjustments using search_emails (query: "invoice", "surcharge", "correction", "approved", "adjustment", or similar). Discovers: (a) Mina Hashimoto's email to Hana about BrightLoop rush fee reduction from $750 to $500 for Simone Richter, (b) Catalina Dubois's forward to Hana confirming Patricia Langford approved all three Canopy Health surcharges on INV-2026-0401: total approved surcharges $2,650, requests removal of pending status (note: Patricia's original approval email lists $850 for Dr. Renata Kovacs, $1,100 for Tyler Greene, and $700 for Priya Natarajan — different amounts AND different people than the actual invoice, which has $850 for Dr. Renata Kovacs, $1,200 for Marcus Webb, and $600 for Tyler Okonkwo; both sets total $2,650. Catalina's forward to Hana strips the itemized list entirely and includes only a generic "Approved on all three surcharge items" with the aggregate $2,650, so Hana never sees the per-item mismatch), (c) Emeka Diallo's email requesting removal of a storage charge he attributes to "Hartley" from the Mosaic Robotics invoice; however, the actual $450 interim storage line on INV-2026-0411 belongs to Keiko Tanaka (8-day Seattle lease gap), not Deshawn Hartley, and (d) Tessa Moreno's email confirming the $500 rush fee agreement with Mina.
OE2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "invoice", "rate", "overcharge", "storage", "billing", or similar) across #finance and #operations channels. Discovers: (a) Alejandro Fuentes's message in #finance flagging that StormCloud Analytics INV-2026-002 was billed at $8,800/relocation vs the negotiated $8,200, resulting in a $600 overcharge × 3 relocations = $1,800 total error, and (b) Chloe Vance's message in #operations noting Mei-Lin Zhao's GreenStack interim hazmat storage should be 7 days, not 11 days, because Derek confirmed the Portland apartment will be ready on April 18 instead of April 22.
OE3: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to find April invoices that need correction. Discovers the current invoices: (a) BrightLoop INV-2026-0308 at $11,350 with $750 rush surcharge, (b) Canopy Health INV-2026-0401 (id: 1009) at $9,210 with three surcharges marked PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL, (c) Mosaic Robotics INV-2026-0411 at $9,010 with three surcharges pending approval: $1,200 custom crating (Zara Okoye), $800 hazmat handling (Keiko Tanaka), and $450 interim storage (Keiko Tanaka, 8-day Seattle lease gap), (d) StormCloud Analytics INV-2026-002 at $26,400 billed at $8,800/relocation, and (e) GreenStack Energy INV-2026-0418 at $9,440 with 11-day interim hazmat storage at $1,650.
OE4: Search Linear comments using linear_list_comments (on relevant issues) or linear_list_issues to find additional invoicing flags. Discovers: (a) Chloe Vance's comment on the Heartland recon issue noting the Canopy Health invoice still shows surcharges as PENDING despite Patricia Langford's April 10 approval, identifying $2,650 in confirmed revenue shown as pending, and (b) Hana Kim's own comment on the same issue acknowledging an invoicing backlog ahead of April close, referencing the Canopy surcharge approval context Chloe flagged, plus separate BrightLoop and Mosaic corrections that reached her directly that week.
OE5: Create a corrected invoice for BrightLoop Analytics using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_brightloop"), reducing the Simone Richter rush surcharge from $750 to $500. Corrected total: $11,100 (was $11,350, reduced by $250).
OE6: Create a corrected invoice for Canopy Health using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_canopy"), updating the three surcharges from PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL to confirmed/approved status per Catalina's forwarded approval (see OE1b). Line items ($850 TN Visa, $1,200 Cold-Chain, $600 Temp Storage) remain as originally invoiced; only the pending status is removed. Total unchanged at $9,210.
OE7: Create a corrected invoice for Mosaic Robotics using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_mosaic"), removing the $450 Keiko Tanaka interim storage line item per Emeka's request (see OE1c). Corrected total: $8,560 (was $9,010, reduced by $450).
OE8: Create a corrected invoice for StormCloud Analytics using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_stormcloud"), changing the per-relocation rate from $8,800 to $8,200. Corrected total: $24,600 (was $26,400, reduced by $1,800).
OE9: Create a corrected invoice for GreenStack Energy using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_greenstack"), reducing the Mei-Lin Zhao interim hazmat storage from 11 days to 7 days at $150/day. Corrected total: $8,840 (was $9,440, reduced by $600).
OE10: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") to confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the reconciliation email.
OE11: Send a reconciliation email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com using send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com) summarizing all five corrected invoices: (1) BrightLoop: rush fee $750→$500, new total $11,100; (2) Canopy Health: surcharges confirmed approved, total unchanged $9,210; (3) Mosaic Robotics: storage line removed, new total $8,560; (4) StormCloud: rate $8,800→$8,200/relocation, new total $24,600; (5) GreenStack: storage 11→7 days, new total $8,840.
OE12: Post a summary in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005") with a recap of all invoice corrections made for April close, giving Alejandro and the finance team visibility into the changes.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Catalina's forward to Hana strips the itemized list entirely and includes only a generic "Approved on all three surcharge items" with the aggregate $2,650, so Hana never sees the per-item mismatch), (c) Emeka Diallo's email requesting removal of a storage charge he attributes to "Hartley" from the Mosaic Robotics invoice; however, the actual $450 interim storage line on INV-2026-0411 belongs to Keiko Tanaka (8-day Seattle lease gap), not Deshawn Hartley, and (d) Tessa Moreno's email confirming the $500 rush fee agreement with Mina.

1 A corrected invoice was created for BrightLoop Analytics (via quickbooks_create_invoice) with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop', reducing the Simone Richter rush surcharge from $750 to $500, resulting in a corrected total of approximately $11,100.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop', doc_number 'INV-2026-0308-R1'. Line items include a Rush Coordination Surcharge at $500 (corrected from $750). The total: $4500 + $500 + $4500 + $1100 + $500 = $11,100 — exactly as required.
✓Run #2A quickbooks_create_invoice call with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop' and doc_number 'INV-2026-0308-CORRECTED' was made. The line items include a rush coordination surcharge of $500 (corrected from $750). The total of all line items ($4500 + $500 + $4500 + $1100 + $500) = $11,100, matching the expected corrected total.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop', doc_number 'INV-2026-0308-R1'. The line items include the Rush Coordination Surcharge at $500 (down from $750). The total of all line items (4500+500+4500+1100+500) equals $11,100.

✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_brightloop', doc_number 'INV-2026-0308-R1', and line items that include the rush surcharge at $500 (down from $750), with a total of $11,100 (4500+500+4500+1100+500). This matches the criterion exactly.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows two quickbooks_create_invoice calls for customer_ref 'cust_brightloop' with doc_number 'INV-2026-0308-R1'. The corrected line items include a Rush Coordination Surcharge of $500 (down from $750), and the total adds up to $4,500 + $500 + $4,500 + $1,100 + $500 = $11,100. This matches the criterion.
2 A corrected invoice was created for Canopy Health (via quickbooks_create_invoice) with customer_ref 'cust_canopy', updating the three surcharges ($850 TN Visa/Customs Broker, $1,200 Cold-Chain Freight, $600 Temporary Storage) from PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL to confirmed/approved status. The total remains $9,210.
outcome

✗Run #2No quickbooks_create_invoice call with customer_ref 'cust_canopy' was made in the trajectory. The agent identified the Canopy Health surcharge approval issue and noted it in the email, but only wrote that 'invoice notes should be updated' without actually creating a corrected invoice in QuickBooks.
✗Run #3The agent did NOT create a corrected invoice for Canopy Health. It treated Canopy as a 'status update only' and noted the surcharges were approved, but no quickbooks_create_invoice call was made with customer_ref 'cust_canopy'.
✗Run #4No quickbooks_create_invoice call with customer_ref 'cust_canopy' was found in the trajectory. The agent treated the Canopy Health correction as a status-only update and did not create a corrected invoice in QuickBooks.
✗Run #5No quickbooks_create_invoice call for customer_ref 'cust_canopy' appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent treated the Canopy Health surcharge status update as a 'status update only' and described it as needing no invoice action, rather than creating a corrected invoice with approved surcharges.
✗Run #6No quickbooks_create_invoice call for customer_ref 'cust_canopy' appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent only mentioned in the email that Canopy's notes needed updating from PENDING to APPROVED but did not create a corrected invoice in QuickBooks.
3 A corrected invoice was created for Mosaic Robotics (via quickbooks_create_invoice) with customer_ref 'cust_mosaic', removing the $450 Keiko Tanaka interim storage line item, resulting in a corrected total of approximately $8,560.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent created INV-2026-0411-R1 for Mosaic Robotics (customer_ref 'cust_mosaic'), but it KEPT the $450 Keiko Tanaka interim storage line item instead of removing it. The agent removed the $1,200 crating surcharge instead. The corrected total in the trajectory is $7,810, not the expected $8,560. The wrong line item was removed.
✗Run #2A quickbooks_create_invoice call for cust_mosaic (INV-2026-0411-CORRECTED) was made, but the $450 Keiko Tanaka interim storage line item was NOT removed — it was retained with 'PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL' status. The agent removed the $1,200 crating surcharge instead. The corrected total is $7,810, not the expected $8,560 (which would result from removing only the $450 storage line).
✗Run #3The agent did call quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_mosaic' (INV-2026-0411-R1), but the corrected invoice STILL INCLUDES the $450 interim storage line for Keiko Tanaka. The agent removed the $1,200 crating surcharge instead, resulting in a total of $7,810 rather than the expected ~$8,560. The $450 Keiko Tanaka storage was not removed.
✗Run #4While the agent did call quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_mosaic' (INV-2026-0411-R1), the $450 Keiko Tanaka interim storage line item was NOT removed — it is still present in the line items. Instead, the agent removed a $1,200 crating surcharge, yielding $7,810 (not the expected $8,560). The criterion requires removing the $450 storage item to reach ~$8,560.
✗Run #5The agent did create a quickbooks_create_invoice for cust_mosaic (INV-2026-0411-R1), but the line items INCLUDE the $450 Keiko Tanaka interim storage (retained), while instead removing the $1,200 Okoye crating surcharge. The criterion requires removal of the $450 storage (resulting in ~$8,560), but the agent's corrected total was $7,810. Wrong correction applied.
✗Run #6The agent created a corrected invoice for cust_mosaic (INV-2026-0411-R1), but the $450 Keiko Tanaka interim storage line item was KEPT, not removed. The total is $7,810 (includes $450 storage), whereas the criterion requires removing that $450 line resulting in a ~$8,560 total. The agent instead removed the $1,200 crating surcharge.
4 A corrected invoice was created for StormCloud Analytics (via quickbooks_create_invoice) with customer_ref 'cust_stormcloud', changing the per-relocation rate from $8,800 to $8,200 across 3 relocations, resulting in a corrected total of approximately $24,600.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent created INV-2026-002-R1 for StormCloud (customer_ref 'cust_stormcloud') with per-relocation rates changed from $8,800 to $8,200 across 3 relocations. However, the agent also applied a $500 goodwill credit simultaneously, resulting in a total of $24,100 (not $24,600 as required). The criterion specifically states the expected corrected total is approximately $24,600.
✗Run #2No quickbooks_create_invoice call with customer_ref 'cust_stormcloud' appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent did not identify or correct the StormCloud Analytics rate overcharge ($8,800 vs $8,200 per relocation).
